posts.text:
National debt just reached a record high of $39T. Trump is bad for the economy & he is trying to distract Americans by scapegoating immigrants & starting a Middle East war. Trump wants your kids to be worse off while his nepo babies live in luxury.

fortune.com/2026/03/18/how-b…

Event Announcement: Zain Lakhani from the Women's Refugee Commission Explains Why ICE Is Deporting Parents Without Their Children. Monday March 23 at 4:30 PM Eastern. Registration required. 

austinkocher.substack.com/p/…

A fair asylum system isn't just humane  it's more efficient, more accurate, and stronger on rule of law. 

Read the full AILA brief: aila.org/library/policy-brie…. 

More analysis at austinkocher.substack.com

AILA's reform recommendations: shift adjudications to USCIS asylum officers, expand legal pathways abroad, reopen ports of entry, and guarantee legal representation. Research shows represented asylum seekers appear in court at a 94% rate.
66% of Americans disapprove of the blanket asylum ban as of early 2026. Public support for asylum has actually increased since Trump took office, even as access has been nearly eliminated.

A new DHS rule will block asylum seekers from obtaining work permits. With 1.4 million cases in backlog, the government estimates it could take up to 173 years to reach the 180-day threshold that triggers eligibility

Third-country deportations exploded from 133 cases in April 2025 to over 12,000 by December 2025. Many deportees were sent to countries with no functioning asylum system, or to El Salvador's CECOT prison.

ICE arrested asylum seekers at immigration court hearings, breaking longstanding courthouse protections. When word spread, people stopped showing up to court and were then ordered removed in absentia.
USCIS halted all asylum adjudications and cut refugee resettlement to 7,500 per year. The 46-year annual average was 98,000. This isn't managing the system, it's dismantling it.

In January 2025, Trump declared a national emergency and ended asylum access at the southern border. Border officials are now ignoring asylum requests outright and expelling seekers, in direct violation of U.S. law.

The U.S. asylum system isn't broken by accident. A new AILA policy brief documents how the Trump administration has systematically dismantled nearly every pathway to protection. Here's what's happening and what a real fix looks like. 🧵

Event Announcement: Zain Lakhani from the Women's Refugee Commission Explains Why ICE Is Deporting Parents Without Their Children

austinkocher.substack.com/p/…
Chuck Norris has spinning roundhouse kicked the bucket.

This research is essential reading for anyone working on immigration policy. 

Read the full paper in International Migration Review: journals.sagepub.com/doi/epu…. 

Learn more: ucpress.edu/books/precarious… 

More immigration data analysis at austinkocher.substack.com

The data gap itself is a policy problem. When the government stops tracking vulnerable children, it becomes impossible to hold agencies accountable or measure the real human cost of enforcement decisions.

Representation rates peaked at 71.8% in 2015 under Obama, then fell steadily through Trump's first term, dropping to 40.3% by 2020, and continued falling under Biden, hitting a record low of 22.2% in 2022.
Where a child lives dramatically shapes their odds. Unaccompanied minors in Republican states had a 43.1% representation rate. In Democratic states, that rate was 61.3%. In Texas, it was just 36.9%.
Children without attorneys were far more likely to be deported. Among children who were granted relief by an immigration judge, 97.9% were represented. Among those deported, only 26.8% had counsel.
Nearly 50% of adjudicated cases ended in a deportation order. Under Trump's first term, that figure rose to 70% of completed cases, compared to 51% under Obama and 31% under Biden.

Only 51% of unaccompanied children had legal representation at any point during their removal proceedings. The youngest children (ages 0 to 4) had the lowest representation rate of all, at just 34.3%.

The national debt just reached a record high of $39T. Trump is bad for the economy & he is trying to distract Americans by scapegoating immigrants & starting a Middle East war. Trump wants your kids to be worse off while his nepo babies live in grifted luxury.

ICE arrested asylum seekers at immigration court hearings, breaking longstanding courthouse protections. When word spread, people stopped showing up to court and were then ordered removed in absentia.
USCIS halted all asylum adjudications and cut refugee resettlement to 7,500 per year. The 46-year annual average was 98,000. This isn't managing the system, it's dismantling it.

In January 2025, Trump declared a national emergency and ended asylum access at the southern border. Border officials are now ignoring asylum requests outright and expelling seekers, in direct violation of U.S. law.

The U.S. asylum system isn't broken by accident. A new AILA policy brief documents how the Trump administration has systematically dismantled nearly every pathway to protection. Here's what's happening and what a real fix looks like. 🧵

“Death in detention is no longer a shocking rarity that drew genuine concern from the agency,” @austinkocher.com writes. “Death in detention becoming a predictable and normalized part of ICE operations while Congress and most of the American public appears unaware or indifferent.” #AbuseofPowerWatch
